sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: The Scarlet Bullet
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Detective Conan: The Scarlet Bullet for the same reason it broke through in 2021: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Chika Nagaoka keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Scarlet Bullet clicked for you, Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: The Scarlet Bullet?
faq.answer: Try Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven, Case Closed: The Fist of Blue Sapphire, Case Closed: The Crimson Love Letter—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: The Scarlet Bullet while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: The Scarlet Bullet?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: The Scarlet Bullet (2021) is best described as Animation, Action, Crime, Mystery, Romance, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: The Scarlet Bullet part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
